hero.badge: Expert Doctor-Led Weight Management in Malaysia
hero.title: Medically Supervised Weight Loss in Malaysia
hero.highlight: Lasting, Structured Results at Nexus Clinic KL
hero.description: Weight is not just about appearance. It affects your heart, joints, hormones, sleep and long-term health. Our doctor-led programmes are built to treat the biology behind weight gain, not just the symptoms.
hero.details: At Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur, every programme is supervised by licensed doctors who review your medical history, assess the root causes of weight gain, and build a plan based on your body, your risk profile and your lifestyle.
hero.note: Programmes available: GLP-1, Duromine, HCG, Wegovy, Ozempic, Mounjaro — All medically supervised at Nexus Clinic KL.

programmeAtGlance.title: Doctor Monitored Weight Loss Programme at a Glance

items.title: Weight Loss Rate
items.text: 0.5 to 1.5 kg per week depending on protocol

items.title: Suitable For
items.text: BMI above 25, obesity-related conditions, failed previous attempts

items.title: Medications
items.text: GLP-1 agonists, Duromine, HCG, Mounjaro, Wegovy, Ozempic

items.title: Surgical Option
items.text: Bariatric referral for eligible BMI 35+ patients

rootCauses.title: Addressing the root causes of weight gain
rootCauses.description1: Modern obesity medicine recognises that weight gain is not a personal failure. It is driven by hormonal, metabolic, neurological, structural and behavioural factors that often require medical intervention to correct.
rootCauses.description2: This is why diet and exercise alone often fail without medical support. Repeated caloric restriction can slow metabolism, increase hunger and encourage rebound weight gain. Doctor-led weight loss works by treating the physiology behind the pattern.
rootCauses.description3: Supervision by an experienced healthcare professional is not optional when these risk factors are present. It is essential. Self-managed weight loss in patients with hypertension, fatty liver, or metabolic syndrome carries real clinical risk if medications are adjusted or caloric restriction is too severe without monitoring. The structured blood tests, vital sign checks, and ongoing doctor oversight built into every programme at Nexus Clinic KL ensure safety at every stage.
rootCauses.toAddressText: to address localised fat deposits that do not respond to weight loss alone.
rootCauses.conclusion: At Nexus Clinic KL, the programme is matched to the causes driving your weight gain, not simply to the number on the scale.

factors.title: Hormonal imbalance
factors.desc: Thyroid issues, PCOS, insulin resistance and cortisol-related fat storage patterns.

factors.title: Metabolic adaptation
factors.desc: Repeated dieting lowers metabolic rate and makes further loss harder.

factors.title: Neurological appetite signals
factors.desc: Leptin and ghrelin dysfunction can drive persistent hunger despite adequate intake.

factors.title: Structural and mobility barriers
factors.desc: Joint pain or disability can limit physical activity and worsen weight gain.

factors.title: Behavioural and environmental factors
factors.desc: Stress, sleep deprivation, emotional eating and Malaysian food culture can all contribute.

factors.title: Medication-induced weight gain
factors.desc: Some antidepressants, steroids and diabetes drugs can worsen excess weight.

patientProfile.title: Choosing the right programme by patient profile
patientProfile.description: Not every patient needs the same strategy. The doctor at Nexus Clinic KL matches your programme to your BMI, medical history, risk factors and previous weight-loss experience.

profiles.title: Mild overweight, no comorbidities
profiles.desc: BMI 25 to 29.9: structured lifestyle plus Duromine or HCG programme.

profiles.title: Moderate obesity, early metabolic issues
profiles.desc: BMI 30 to 34.9: GLP-1 programme like Ozempic or Saxenda with dietary coaching.

profiles.title: Significant obesity, hypertension or fatty liver
profiles.desc: BMI 35 to 39.9: Mounjaro or Wegovy plus metabolic workup and meal planning.

profiles.title: Severe obesity, multiple risk factors
profiles.desc: BMI 40 and above: intensive GLP-1 protocol or bariatric surgery evaluation.

profiles.title: Weight plateau after previous programme
profiles.desc: Protocol switch, dose escalation or selective adjunct body contouring discussion.

profiles.title: Post-bariatric maintenance
profiles.desc: Medical supervision, nutrition monitoring and hormonal support where needed.

professionalGuidance.title: Why professional guidance matters
professionalGuidance.description: Obesity increases the risk of type 2 diabetes, hypertension, heart disease, fatty liver disease, sleep apnoea, joint pain and reduced mobility. In these cases, self-directed weight loss is not enough. It needs monitoring.
protectionItems: Monitoring of blood pressure, pulse and metabolic markers
Adjustment of existing diabetes or hypertension medicines
Early detection of side effects or nutritional issues
professionalGuidance.glp1LinkText: full GLP-1 programme at Nexus Clinic
professionalGuidance.studyReference: Wilding JPH et al. Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity. The New England Journal of Medicine. 2021;384:989-1002. The STEP 1 trial demonstrated 14.9% mean body weight loss with semaglutide versus 2.4% with placebo, confirming the significant impact of GLP-1 receptor agonism on medically supervised weight reduction.
professionalGuidance.linksPrefix: Patients interested in specific GLP-1 programmes can learn more about

medications.title: Weight loss medications at Nexus Clinic KL
medications.description: GLP-1 medications and other prescription tools have transformed medically supervised weight loss in Malaysia. These are used only after consultation and assessment.

list.name: Ozempic
list.desc: GLP-1 weekly injection, diabetes plus weight management

list.name: Mounjaro
list.desc: Dual GIP/GLP-1 weekly injection, maximum metabolic impact

list.name: Saxenda
list.desc: Daily GLP-1 injection, often first-time GLP-1 pathway

list.name: Duromine
list.desc: Daily oral appetite suppressant for shorter-term use

bariatric.title: Bariatric surgery: when surgery becomes the right choice
bariatric.description1: For patients with BMI 35 or above, especially with comorbidities like type 2 diabetes, severe hypertension or fatty liver disease, bariatric surgery may be the most clinically appropriate pathway.
bariatric.description2: Nexus Clinic KL does not perform bariatric surgery directly, but it can provide honest guidance, referral, pre-operative support and post-bariatric maintenance care when surgery is proportionate and necessary.

stepByStep.title: The doctor monitored programme at Nexus Clinic KL

steps.title: Step 1: Initial consultation and medical history review
steps.desc: A 45 to 60 minute consultation covering your weight history, medications, lifestyle and relevant comorbidities.

steps.title: Step 2: Blood tests and body composition analysis
steps.desc: Baseline testing may include blood count, kidney and liver function, fasting glucose, HbA1c, lipid profile, thyroid panel and selected hormonal markers.

steps.title: Step 3: Personalised treatment plan
steps.desc: Your doctor builds a written plan covering medication pathway, meal structure, physical activity targets and follow-up schedule before any prescription is issued.

steps.title: Step 4: Treatment commencement and close monitoring
steps.desc: Injection training is provided where needed. Weekly or fortnightly check-ins track weight, side effects, vital signs and progress toward targets.

steps.title: Step 5: Review, adjust and support
steps.desc: The programme is adjusted over time based on body composition, metabolic markers, plateaus, side effects and changes in health status.

pricing.title: Medical weight loss clinic pricing in Malaysia 2026
pricing.description: The cost of a medically supervised weight loss programme at Nexus Clinic KL depends on the protocol chosen, the medications prescribed, and the duration of your programme. All first consultations are complimentary. Blood tests and medications are priced transparently and itemised on your treatment plan before you commit. There are no hidden package fees. The table below reflects current 2026 pricing as a reference.
pricing.descriptionSuffix: for body contouring may qualify for combined programme pricing. Your doctor will discuss all options at consultation.

safety.title: Professional guidance, patient safety, and what to expect
safety.description1: All programmes at Nexus Clinic KL are supervised by qualified doctors trained in obesity medicine and metabolic health. Blood tests may be repeated during treatment to monitor liver, kidney and metabolic markers. Blood pressure and pulse are checked regularly.
safety.description2: GLP-1 medications can cause nausea, bloating and loose stools during dose titration. Duromine patients are monitored for heart rate and blood pressure effects. HCG protocol patients are monitored for energy levels and electrolyte balance.
safety.safetyNoteText: NPRA has published updated safety alerts on GLP-1 medicines, including semaglutide and tirzepatide, concerning aspiration and aspiration pneumonia risk during general anaesthesia or deep sedation. Patients must tell their doctor and anaesthetist if they are taking these medicines before any procedure.
safety.description3: The support you receive at Nexus Clinic KL extends beyond prescriptions. Your doctor provides guidance on building healthy habits that outlast the medication phase. The aim is always to help you maintain your results and avoid the cycle of weight loss followed by rapid regain that plagues unmonitored approaches.
safety.hormoneTestLinkText: hormone test and evaluation in Malaysia
safety.toRuleOutText: to rule out underlying hormonal contributors to weight difficulty.

results.title: What results can you expect?
results.description: Weight loss is usually gradual. The right programme aims for steady, sustainable progress rather than short-term extremes.
results.studyText: In the STEP 1 trial, once-weekly semaglutide with lifestyle support produced an average weight loss of
results.studyComparison: versus 2.4% with placebo. Real-world results still vary by protocol, adherence, starting weight and metabolic profile.

comparisons.title: Doctor monitored weight loss
comparisons.desc: Best for overall fat loss, appetite control, metabolic health and long-term maintenance.

comparisons.title: Body contouring
comparisons.desc: Best for stubborn fat pockets and shape refinement, not for large overall weight reduction.

comparisons.title: Bariatric surgery
comparisons.desc: Best for severe obesity and more complex medical risk profiles, under specialist care.

items.q: How is doctor monitored weight loss different from commercial slimming centres?
items.a: A medical weight loss clinic operates under medical licensing with licensed doctors who can prescribe, adjust, and supervise treatment, conduct blood tests, and manage health conditions that complicate weight loss. Commercial slimming centres cannot provide the same clinical supervision.

items.q: Do I need to follow a strict diet and exercise plan during the programme?
items.a: Diet and exercise still matter, but the programme is designed to be realistic rather than rigid. Nutrition and activity guidance are adapted to Malaysian food culture, lifestyle, and any mobility limitations.

items.q: Am I a candidate for GLP-1 weight loss medications like Ozempic or Mounjaro?
items.a: GLP-1 medications are generally considered for adults with BMI 27 or above plus at least one weight-related medical condition, or BMI 30 or above without comorbidities. Full medical consultation and blood tests are required before prescription.

items.q: How much weight can I expect to lose on a medically supervised programme?
items.a: Results vary by starting weight, protocol, adherence and metabolic profile. As a broad guide, Duromine or HCG programmes may produce a few kilograms per month, while GLP-1 programmes and tirzepatide-based programmes can produce much larger percentage reductions over longer timelines.

items.q: Is bariatric surgery something Nexus Clinic KL offers or can refer for?
items.a: Nexus Clinic KL does not perform bariatric surgery directly but can provide medically guided preparation, maintenance support, and referral to accredited bariatric specialists in Kuala Lumpur when surgery is the appropriate option.

items.q: What blood tests are done as part of the weight loss programme?
items.a: The initial blood panel may include full blood count, kidney and liver function, fasting glucose, HbA1c, lipid profile, thyroid markers, and selected hormonal markers when clinically indicated.

items.q: Can I lose weight if I have a hormonal condition like hypothyroidism or PCOS?
items.a: Yes, but the programme must account for the hormonal driver. Nexus Clinic KL screens for issues like thyroid dysfunction, insulin resistance and PCOS-related metabolic problems before finalising the treatment plan.

items.q: How long do I need to stay on weight loss medications?
items.a: The answer depends on the medication and your clinical response. GLP-1 programmes are often longer-term because obesity is a chronic condition and stopping treatment too early can lead to regain without a proper maintenance strategy.

items.q: Is the weight loss programme safe for patients with hypertension or diabetes?
items.a: Yes, and in many cases it is especially appropriate because even modest weight loss can improve blood pressure, glucose control and cardiovascular risk. These patients are monitored more closely during active treatment.

items.q: How do I get started with a weight loss programme at Nexus Clinic KL?
items.a: The process starts with a complimentary first consultation at Nexus Clinic KL, followed by medical assessment, blood tests when needed, and a personalised plan before treatment begins.
